vision_statement: Decifer is a cloud-hosted, multi-user profitable trading bot where friends and family connect via a web interface, link their own IBKR accounts, and manage risk through personal settings. The path runs from paper trading profitability through live single-account trading to a hosted multi-user platform.
current_stage_assessment: Decifer is in paper_trading_single_account stage. Recent commits show active stabilisation work (bug fixes, dedup logic, logging improvements, test API reconciliation) alongside the first multi-account scaffolding (Phase E position aggregation). The bot appears to be running and generating paper trades, but test infrastructure shows 0% pass rate due to network/connection errors in CI rather than logic failures — indicating the test environment is not mocked or isolated properly. The next priority must be proving paper trading profitability before any live or cloud work begins.
executive_summary: Decifer has meaningful signal infrastructure (9 dimensions, direction-agnostic scoring, short scanner, regime gates) and a functioning paper trading loop, but the project is carrying serious sequencing risk: 50 active features span everything from signal quality to multi-user Docker containers, with no clear gating on profitability first. The test suite shows 0 passing tests — not because logic is broken but because network calls are hitting real endpoints in tests, which is a structural gap that will mask real regressions. The feature pipeline contains several near-duplicate items (IC-Weighted Signal Scoring appears twice, Connection Manager appears in both active and backlog, Reddit Surge Detector appears twice) suggesting spec management has drifted. The strongest near-term leverage is in the P1 signal quality cluster (multi-timeframe confirmation, regime-gated routing, Z-score normalisation, options flow) because research consistently shows these are the highest-lift improvements for win rate before going live. Cloud and multi-user work (MA1-MA3) should not begin until a single-account paper trading edge is demonstrated.

product_roadmap.goal: Achieve a reliable, passing test suite with all external I/O mocked so regressions are detectable and CI is trustworthy.
product_roadmap.rationale: 0% pass rate means the project is flying blind. Every subsequent feature addition risks silently breaking existing behaviour. This must be fixed before any signal or architecture work so that changes can be validated with confidence.
features: Mock all yfinance, AlphaVantage, Stooq, and IBKR network calls in tests using unittest.mock or pytest fixtures
Fix data_collector test harness — stub HTTP responses for all four data sources
Fix orders.py test harness — stub IBKR openTrades(), reqGlobalCancel(), and flatten order flow
Establish baseline pass rate target (100% of existing tests green before any new feature merges)
Add CI gate: tests must pass before any commit lands on main
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: All existing tests pass with mocked I/O. No ERROR-level log entries from network calls during test runs. CI gate is enforced.

product_roadmap.goal: Improve signal quality and composite scoring so the paper trading bot has a demonstrable edge before any live capital is risked.
product_roadmap.rationale: Research findings confirm IC-weighted dynamic scoring and regime-conditional routing are the highest-leverage improvements. These must be in place and validated on paper before live trading begins. The current equal-weight static composite is the weakest link in the alpha chain.
features: Z-Score Normalised Signal Composite — cross-sectional standardisation before compositing
Multi-Timeframe Signal Confirmation — daily/4H/1H alignment gate
Regime-Gated Momentum / Mean-Reversion Signal Router — VIX proxy two-state regime detector
Options Flow Proxy Signal via Put/Call Ratio — yfinance options chain, new signal dimension
Unusual Options Activity (UOA) Signal Dimension — per Dr. Cuyler Strong research
IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scorer — replace equal-weight composite with rolling IC weights (consolidate the two duplicate specs into one implementation)
Signal Decay Monitor & Freshness Dashboard — weekly rolling IC per dimension
Alpha Decay Chart — per-trade forward return distribution tracker
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Paper trading composite signal uses IC-weighted, Z-score normalised, regime-gated scoring. Rolling IC dashboard shows at least 4 of 9 dimensions with positive IC over a 30-day window. Signal Decay Monitor is running and logging weekly.

product_roadmap.goal: Ensure position sizing, drawdown management, and execution quality are robust enough for live trading.
product_roadmap.rationale: Research findings flag plain Kelly as dangerous for live systems. Before a single dollar of live capital is committed, the sizing and drawdown brake systems must be in place and validated on paper. Execution cost modelling is required to accurately assess paper P&L.
features: ATR Volatility-Targeting Position Size Layer — volatility-targeting formula replacing or capping static sizing
VIX-Rank Adaptive Fractional Kelly Sizer — dynamic Kelly fraction, replaces static
Drawdown Brake — Tiered Equity Curve Circuit Breaker — three-tier auto-reduction
Dual-Tranche Exit Strategy — split positions into two tranches on entry
Correlation-Aware Position Management — 20-day rolling correlation matrix, reduce correlated exposure
Portfolio Heat Monitor with Correlation-Adjusted Exposure Cap — pre-order correlation-adjusted exposure check
Anchored VWAP Entry Gate — weekly AVWAP binary gate
Multi-Timeframe Momentum Confirmation Filter — daily/weekly/monthly momentum alignment
Slippage & Execution Cost Model for Backtester — realistic per-trade slippage based on ADTV
Walk-Forward Backtesting — rolling 6mo train / 1mo test windows
Monte Carlo Stress Testing — 1000x trade sequence shuffle for drawdown distribution
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Paper trading shows positive Sharpe (>0.5) over 60+ trading days with drawdown brake never triggered more than twice. Walk-forward backtest validates edge across at least 3 out-of-sample windows. Slippage model is applied to all paper P&L calculations.

product_roadmap.goal: Transition from paper to live trading on a single IBKR account with full safety rails active.
product_roadmap.rationale: Only after demonstrated paper profitability and hardened risk management should real capital be deployed. This phase is the live_trading_single_account milestone.
features: Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop — typed Signal objects separate from execution (MA1 P0 / B P1 duplicate — one implementation)
Regime-Conditional Signal Weighting — ensure live regime gate is active
Time-of-Day Seasonality Filter — avoid low-quality execution windows
Order Flow Imbalance Detection — institutional accumulation proxy
Live paper-to-live account switch with kill switch and manual override
Confirm all capital safety checks (position limits, drawdown brake, correlation guard) are enforced on live orders
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Bot executes live trades on a single IBKR account. Drawdown brake has been tested and fires correctly in staging. At least 30 live trades completed with P&L tracked and matching expected slippage model. No critical order errors in production logs for 14 days.

product_roadmap.goal: Move the single-account bot from local to a cloud VPS so it runs 24/7 without developer machine dependency.
product_roadmap.rationale: This is the cloud_hosted_single_account milestone. Must be stable at single-account live trading before adding cloud complexity.
features: Cloud VPS Setup and Deployment — Ubuntu 22, Docker, shared brain container (consolidate MA2 and B P2 duplicate specs)
Reconnection Logic — Backoff, Sequence, State Sync — exponential backoff, state sync on reconnect (consolidate MA2 and C P2 duplicates)
Telegram Bot — Notifications and Quick Commands — trade notifications, morning brief, emergency stop (consolidate MA3 and B P2 duplicates)
Per-Account Local Dashboard (cloud-accessible) — positions, P&L, trade history on port 8080
Automated restart on crash with alerting
Secrets management for IBKR credentials in cloud environment
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Bot runs continuously on cloud VPS for 14 days with no manual restarts. Telegram notifications fire on every trade. Dashboard accessible remotely. Reconnection logic survives a simulated network drop.

product_roadmap.goal: Extend the cloud-hosted bot to support multiple users, each with their own IBKR account, risk profile, and isolated container.
product_roadmap.rationale: This is the multi_user_beta milestone. All MA-tagged backlog items belong here. Must not begin until the single-account cloud system is proven stable.
features: Per-Account Config Profiles — account-aware profile system replacing flat CONFIG (consolidate MA1 P0 and B P2 duplicates)
AccountDecisionAgent Class — one instance per IBKR sub-account (consolidate MA1 P0 and B P2 duplicates)
Connection Manager Multi-Account IBKR — multiple ib_insync instances (consolidate MA1 P0 and B P2 duplicates)
Parameterise account_id Through Orders and Risk — audit and replace all CONFIG['active_account'] reads (consolidate MA1 P0 and B P2 duplicates)
Per-Account Risk Profile Config Schema with Multi-Tier Presets — RiskProfile dataclass
Per-User Docker Compose Containers with Encrypted Credentials
WebSocket Signal Broadcaster — brain publishes signals to all connected containers (consolidate MA2 and B P1 duplicates)
Signal Registry — Per-User Favourite Symbols
Per-User Dashboard Authentication — login, sessions, isolation
One-Time User Setup Screen — guided IBKR credential and risk profile setup
Web Dashboard — Live Positions and P&L per user (consolidate MA3 and B P2 duplicates)
Per-Account Local Dashboard update for multi-user context
Cross-Account Correlation Guard
Unified Trade Journal — cross-account with account tags
Account Performance Comparison Dashboard
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: At least 3 users (founder + 2 friends/family) running independently on the platform with isolated containers, separate IBKR accounts, and independent risk profiles. Each user can see only their own data. All accounts have been live trading for at least 14 days without cross-account interference.

product_roadmap.goal: Harden the platform for broader use, add advanced alpha sources, and complete the remaining research-backed signal improvements.
product_roadmap.rationale: This is the multi_user_production milestone. Advanced features like ML meta-labeling, HMM regime detection, and sentiment pipelines add complexity and require a stable platform foundation.
features: Meta-Labeling Secondary Classifier — binary ML meta-model on top of primary signal pipeline
HMM Regime Detection — 3-state HMM on vol + RSI (consolidate D P2 duplicates)
Regime-Conditioned Position Size Scalar — HMM integration with position sizing
Claude Sentiment Scoring Pipeline — Yahoo RSS + Finviz via Claude as 10th signal dimension
News Sentiment Regression Scorer (FinBERT) — arXiv:2507.03350 approach
SEC Form 4 Insider Cluster Buying Dimension — EDGAR RSS free feed
Reddit Mention Velocity Dimension — quantitative surge detector (consolidate two duplicate Reddit specs)
Triple Barrier Method for ML Training Labels — replace binary up/down labels
IC-Weighted Walk-Forward Weight Calibration
Signal Validation with Alphalens — IC scores, decay curves per dimension
Signal Correlation De-Duplication Guard
Rolling CVaR Dashboard Metric and Per-Account Risk Budget
Per-User Risk Limits enforcement
Account-Aware Order Router
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Platform supports 5+ concurrent users. All advanced signal dimensions are live and IC-validated. HMM regime detection is active and influencing position sizing. Platform has run continuously for 30 days with no critical incidents.

immediate_next_action.action: Fix the test suite — mock all external network calls so tests pass reliably in CI.
immediate_next_action.rationale: The test suite currently shows 0 passing tests entirely due to unmocked network calls (yfinance, AlphaVantage, Stooq, IBKR). This means every feature change is unverifiable and silent regressions are invisible. No other work — signal improvements, risk hardening, or architecture changes — can be trusted without a green test baseline. This is the single highest-leverage action because it unblocks everything else.
immediate_next_action.cowork_prompt:
We need to fix the Decifer test suite. Currently all tests are failing due to unmocked external network calls — yfinance, AlphaVantage, Stooq, and IBKR connections are hitting real endpoints during tests. Here is what I need you to do:

1. Audit all 32 test files and identify every test that makes a real network call to yfinance, AlphaVantage, Stooq, or IBKR.
2. Add proper mock/stub fixtures for each — use unittest.mock.patch or pytest monkeypatch as appropriate. Return realistic dummy data structures that match what the real APIs return.
3. Specifically fix the four data_collector errors (yf-intraday, yf-daily, alphavantage, stooq — all for AAPL) and the four orders.py errors (openTrades, reqGlobalCancel, flatten IBKR connection, flatten order rejected).
4. Run the full test suite and confirm all tests pass.
5. Do not change any production logic — only fix the test harness.

We want 100% of existing tests passing with fully mocked I/O before any new features are added.

untracked_ideas.title: Paper Trading Profitability Report — Formal Go/No-Go Gate
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision (paper trading must be profitable before live trading) and current stage definition
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is no spec or feature for a formal evaluation process that determines when paper trading results are good enough to justify moving to live. Without this gate, the transition to live trading could happen prematurely. A minimum threshold (e.g. Sharpe > 0.5, win rate > 52%, max drawdown < 15% over 60 trading days) should be defined and tracked.

untracked_ideas.title: Duplicate Feature Spec Consolidation — Housekeeping Sprint
untracked_ideas.source: Observed in feature pipeline — IC-Weighted Signal Scoring (two entries), Connection Manager (active + backlog), Reddit Surge Detector (two entries), Cloud VPS (active + backlog), Reconnection Logic (active + backlog), WebSocket Broadcaster (active + backlog), Web Dashboard (active + backlog), Telegram Bot (active + backlog), Per-Account Dashboard (active + backlog), AccountDecisionAgent (active + backlog), Per-Account Config Profiles (active + backlog), Parameterise account_id (active + backlog), Extract Signal Objects (active + backlog)
untracked_ideas.rationale: At least 13 features appear in both Active and Backlog with slightly different descriptions. This creates confusion about which spec is canonical, risks duplicate implementation effort, and makes the pipeline unreliable as a planning tool. A consolidation pass is needed to designate one spec per feature and archive the duplicate.
untracked_ideas.suggested_phase: Phase 1 — Test Infrastructure Hardening (do alongside, low effort)

untracked_ideas.title: Live vs Paper Trading Toggle with Audit Log
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision — transition from paper to live trading must be controlled and reversible
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is no spec for the mechanism that switches the bot from paper to live mode. This needs to be explicit, logged, and reversible — a config flag with an audit trail, not an ad-hoc code change. Should include a confirmation step and automatic notification.

untracked_ideas.title: Broker Account Verification Flow — IBKR Credential Validation on Setup
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by multi-user vision — One-Time User Setup Screen exists but no spec for credential validation
untracked_ideas.rationale: When friends and family connect their IBKR accounts, the system needs to verify the connection works, the account has sufficient capital, and paper/live mode is correctly configured before any bot activity starts. A failed connection should produce a clear error, not a silent failure.

untracked_ideas.title: Emergency Kill Switch — Global and Per-User
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision (safety for friends and family capital) and current orders.py flatten logic
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is flatten/global cancel logic in orders.py but no explicit product spec for a user-facing emergency stop — both global (all accounts) and per-user. This is a critical safety feature that should exist before any live trading. The Telegram bot mentions an 'emergency stop' command but it is not specified as a standalone safety feature.

untracked_ideas.title: Performance Attribution Dashboard — Which Signals Are Generating P&L
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by IC-weighted signal research and the Alpha Decay / Signal Decay Monitor features
untracked_ideas.rationale: As the signal pipeline grows to 9-10 dimensions with IC weighting, there is no spec for showing the founder which specific signal dimensions have contributed most to closed-trade P&L. This is distinct from IC (forward return correlation) — it is actual realised attribution. Critical for knowing what to keep, tune, and cut.

untracked_ideas.title: Rate Limiting and API Cost Management for External Data Sources
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by adding Claude Sentiment, FinBERT, SEC EDGAR, Reddit, and yfinance simultaneously
untracked_ideas.rationale: With 5+ external data sources planned, there is no spec for managing API rate limits, costs (especially Claude), or fallback behaviour when a source is unavailable. Without this, adding sentiment and alternative data sources risks API bans, unexpected costs, or silent data gaps that corrupt signal quality.

risks.risk: 0% test pass rate means regressions are invisible — any code change could silently break trading logic and no automated check would catch it
risks.mitigation: Immediately mock all external I/O in tests (Phase 1). Enforce CI gate: no merge to main unless all tests pass. This is the single highest-priority action.

risks.risk: 50 active features with no clear prioritisation gate on paper trading profitability — the project could reach multi-user architecture work before the core bot has proven it can make money
risks.mitigation: Define and enforce a formal Go/No-Go gate with quantitative paper trading thresholds (Sharpe, win rate, max drawdown over minimum observation period) before Phase 4 work begins. Freeze MA1-MA3 architecture work until gate is passed.

risks.risk: 13+ duplicate feature specs across Active and Backlog create planning ambiguity — risk of double implementation or conflicting specs being built simultaneously
risks.mitigation: Conduct a spec consolidation sprint to designate one canonical spec per feature and archive duplicates. Apply a naming convention to prevent future duplication. Can be done alongside Phase 1 test work.

risks.risk: bot.py at 2787 lines and dashboard.py at 2149 lines — monolithic modules with high change frequency risk merge conflicts, hard-to-test logic, and cascading failures
risks.mitigation: The Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop feature (already in pipeline) directly addresses bot.py bloat. Prioritise this in Phase 2. Dashboard should be split into API layer and UI layer as part of Phase 5 cloud work.

risks.risk: Friends and family capital will be managed by the live bot — a single risk management bug (sizing error, position limit bypass, runaway order) could cause significant real losses and relationship damage
risks.mitigation: All three risk layers (ATR sizing, drawdown brake, correlation guard) must be implemented AND tested against edge cases before any live trading begins. Add an emergency kill switch spec. Require 60 days of clean paper trading with no brake triggers before going live.

risks.risk: Multi-user architecture (Docker, WebSocket, per-user containers) is being designed in parallel with early paper trading signal work — premature architecture complexity before the alpha is validated
risks.mitigation: Strictly sequence phases. No MA1-MA3 implementation work until Phase 4 live trading is stable. Use the current single-account architecture as long as possible. Do not let cloud architecture work crowd out signal quality work.

risks.risk: IBKR connection reliability — orders.py logs show FLATTEN failures, reqGlobalCancel rejections, and connection loss errors. If this happens in live trading with real capital, positions could be stranded
risks.mitigation: Reconnection Logic (backoff, state sync) must be completed and stress-tested before live trading. Add explicit test coverage for IBKR disconnection scenarios. Define the bot's behaviour when it cannot confirm a position is closed.

risks.risk: No session logs yet — without recorded development context, product decisions are not traceable and it is impossible to know why specific implementation choices were made
risks.mitigation: Begin logging Cowork session summaries in the project after each significant session. The CLAUDE.md governance file is a good start — extend it to capture decision rationale.

roadmap_gaps: Several gaps exist between the current feature pipeline and the full vision. (1) No formal paper trading profitability evaluation process or Go/No-Go gate is specified anywhere — the transition to live trading lacks a defined trigger. (2) No emergency kill switch spec exists as a standalone feature despite being implied by every live trading stage. (3) No credential validation or IBKR connection health check flow is specified for the user onboarding path. (4) No rate limiting or API cost management spec exists despite 5+ external data sources being planned. (5) No performance attribution spec exists — which signals are actually generating closed-trade P&L is distinct from IC and is currently untracked. (6) The live-to-paper trading toggle mechanism has no spec — how the bot switches modes, with what audit trail, and by whom is undefined. (7) There is no user notification system spec for risk events (drawdown brake fired, correlation guard triggered, position limit hit) separate from trade execution notifications. (8) The backtester's relationship to live signal validation is unclear — there is no spec for a continuous validation loop that re-runs the backtester as new market data arrives and alerts if edge has degraded. (9) No data retention or GDPR/privacy consideration spec exists for when the platform holds multiple users' trading data and broker credentials. (10) No spec exists for handling IBKR API version updates or TWS/Gateway version compatibility, which is a recurring operational risk for IBKR-dependent bots.
